summary.intent: Address new PR review comments on the moltis external-agents/sessions integration, specifically fixing a raw String type for external_agent_kind and ensuring tracing instrumentation is applied to all runtime stubs.
summary.outcome: Added a typed ExternalAgentKind enum to the sessions crate replacing the raw String field, and applied #[tracing::instrument] to all 5 runtime stub implementations. All 99 tests pass, clippy and format clean, changes committed and pushed.

repo: The sessions crate has no dependency on external-agents, requiring a mirror enum (ExternalAgentKind) in sessions for typed agent kind representation at the serialization boundary.
The sessions crate defines a crate::Result alias that shadows std::result::Result, requiring explicit std::result::Result<_, _> in FromStr impls to avoid type conflicts.
Tracing instrumentation on runtime stubs must be feature-gated with #[cfg_attr(feature = "tracing", tracing::instrument(...))].
The workspace has 9 external-agents tests and 90 sessions tests (99 total).

code.finding: external_agent_kind field changed from Option<String> to Option<ExternalAgentKind> with a typed enum and FromStr impl; required std::result::Result to avoid shadowing by crate::Result alias.
ExternalAgentKind re-exported from the sessions crate public API.
All 5 runtime files (claude_code, opencode, codex, pi_agent, acp) updated with feature-gated tracing::instrument attributes.

workflow: PR reviewer bots may re-flag comments against old commits even after fixes are pushed; cross-referencing commit IDs in comment metadata helps identify which comments are truly new.
When a reviewer flags a doc-comment workaround, prefer the typed solution they expect rather than doubling down on the comment.

friction: crate::Result alias in sessions crate caused a compile error in the new FromStr impl, requiring an explicit std::result::Result workaround.
Reviewer re-flagged the tracing instrumentation despite it already being present on registry — caused ambiguity about scope of the fix needed.
